title: Microsoft previews its Agent Framework for Go
friendlyTitle: Microsoft brings AI agent tools to Go programmers
summary: Microsoft has released a public preview of its Agent Framework SDK for Go. It brings tool use, MCP, multi-agent workflows, checkpoints, approvals, and OpenTelemetry tracing to Go services and workers.
friendlySummary: Microsoft has a preview toolkit for people who write software in Go. It helps them build AI agents that can use tools, work together, and keep a record of what happened.
whyItMatters: Go is common in infrastructure and backend systems, so a native agent framework could make production-grade AI workflows easier to add where teams already run their services.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can help teams add AI features to the Go services they already use, without rebuilding everything in another language.
